content: We can hypothesize that integrating multiple inconsistent problem-solving perspectives from LLM self-reflection mechanisms into the Self-Refine framework creates a compounding self-improvement loop: each iteration of Self-Refine not only revises the initial output based on self-feedback, but also leverages conflicting perspectives pulled from self-reflection to surface hidden flaws that single-perspective self-feedback would miss. This hybrid framework retains Self-Refine’s core advantage of requiring no extra training or labeled data while addressing its key limitation of getting stuck in local optima from homogeneous self-feedback.
